PROFILE

About Me

I'm Eslam Nabil Mohamed, an Aerospace and Mechanical Engineering student at Zewail City of Science and Technology — Egypt's foremost research institution, founded by Nobel Laureate Ahmed Zewail.

My engineering focus sits at the intersection of propulsion systems, flight dynamics, and control theory. I'm driven by the challenge of translating abstract physics into systems that actually fly — whether that's a microjet engine machined in the lab, a pitch autopilot tuned on root locus plots, or a rocket interception scenario solved in MATLAB.

I approach every project with an engineer's discipline: rigorous analysis first, then simulation, then hardware. I believe aerospace pushes mathematics and creativity to their absolute limit — and that's precisely why I chose it.

My long-term goal is to contribute to advanced propulsion development or autonomous flight systems at a world-class aerospace organization.

EDUCATION

Academic Background

Undergraduate
Zewail City of Science and Technology
Bachelor of Science in Aerospace and Mechanical Engineering
Sharqia, Egypt · 2022 – Present

Zewail City is Egypt's foremost research institution, founded by Nobel Laureate Ahmed Zewail. The curriculum integrates rigorous fundamental sciences with hands-on engineering, producing graduates with deep analytical capability and practical design experience. Coursework spans classical mechanics, thermodynamics, control theory, numerical methods, and experimental lab practice.

ENGINEERING WORK

Featured Projects

PROJECT_01 Featured In Fabrication
Microjet Engine Design & Fabrication
Full design-to-fabrication project spanning thermodynamic cycle analysis, SolidWorks CAD for all turbomachinery components, and hands-on manufacturing of a single-shaft turbojet. The engine architecture includes a centrifugal compressor, annular combustor, and axial turbine stage. Currently in the precision machining phase with compressor and turbine blades under production.
Architecture
Single-shaft turbojet
Design Tool
SolidWorks CAD/CAM
Stages
Compressor · Combustor · Turbine
Analysis
Thermodynamic cycle + CFD
SolidWorks Thermodynamics Turbomachinery CAD/CAM Manufacturing Combustion
CAD / Wireframe Render
Live Test — Afterburner
PROJECT_02
Aircraft Longitudinal Pitch Autopilot
Closed-loop pitch control system designed via Root Locus and PID techniques in MATLAB/Simulink. Validated transient performance against rise time, overshoot, and settling time specifications from FAA requirements.
MATLABSimulink Root LocusPID Control
PROJECT_03
650 MW Regenerative Reheat Rankine Cycle
MATLAB simulation of a large-scale steam power plant using XSteam. Parametric sweeps over extraction pressures and feedwater heater configurations to maximize cycle thermal efficiency.
MATLABXSteam ThermodynamicsOptimization
PROJECT_04
Cantilever Beam Vibration Analysis
Experimental modal testing to identify natural frequencies and damping ratios of a cantilever beam. Compared analytical predictions vs. FFT-processed accelerometer data.
Vibration AnalysisModal Testing MATLABSignal Processing
PROJECT_05
Rocket Guidance & Trajectory Simulation
Nonlinear 6-DOF MATLAB simulation for missile interception. Implemented Runge-Kutta integration, drag models, and Proportional Navigation guidance laws to analyse miss distance under realistic flight conditions.
MATLABRunge-Kutta PN Guidance6-DOF Sim
PROJECT_06
EURUS Horizontal Blade Competition
Aerodynamic design and structural optimisation of a horizontal-axis rotor blade using Blade Element Momentum theory. Weight-performance tradeoffs evaluated against competition regulations.
AerodynamicsBEM Theory Structural Opt.Competition
